about.title: About Nigehbaan
about.missionText: Nigehbaan ("guardian" in Urdu) is an open-source geospatial intelligence platform designed to shine a light on child trafficking patterns across Pakistan. By aggregating data from media reports, court records, NGO assessments, and citizen reports, Nigehbaan empowers researchers, journalists, law enforcement, and civil society to identify at-risk areas and advocate for policy change.

footer.copyright: Nigehbaan Project — Open Source Intelligence for Child Protection

maker.title: Meet the Maker
maker.subtitle: The guy who creates stuff
maker.name: Hassan Arshad
maker.bioLine1: I'm Hassan — a sophomore studying Computer Engineering at Habib University who spends way too much time building things.
maker.bioLine2: I founded Zensbot, an AI/tech agency that also builds its own products. Nigehbaan started because I wanted to see how I can help — Pakistan's child trafficking data is scattered across 90+ sources, locked in PDFs, not geocoded, not connected. So I built a platform to change that.
maker.bioLine3: When I'm not coding, I'm probably still coding.
maker.nigehbaanDesc: Open-source child trafficking intelligence platform for Pakistan. Aggregates 90+ data sources, maps every incident, and empowers those who protect children. You're looking at it.
maker.qanoonaiDesc: AI-powered legal research assistant for Pakistani law — court judgments, statutes, and precedents at your fingertips.
maker.ictlmsDesc: Open-source learning management system built for modern classrooms. Course delivery, assessments, and analytics.
maker.zenscallDesc: AI voice agent that handles phone calls, books appointments, and never puts you on hold.
